raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing two ( 2 ) unauthorized baggage charges applied to my Barclays credit card in connection with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ). Despite multiple submissions of documentation, Barclays has repeatedly denied my dispute, claiming the charges were applied correctly. That position is unsupported by the facts. 
Facts of the Case 1. In our group, there were three passengers. One of the travelers was on a separate itinerary, yet he was also charged the same standard amount for his first checked bag. This further confirms that each passenger only checked in one bag and that the first-bag fee was applied consistently. Therefore, the additional charges labeled second and additional are incorrect, as no extra bags were checked in.. 
XXXX. XXXX incorrectly added : One Second Bag charge ( {$120.00} USD ) ( XXXX ) One Additional Bag charge ( {$260.00} USD ) ( XXXX ) XXXX. These bags did not exist, and no documentation ( e.g., baggage tags or weight logs ) has ever been provided to support these charges. 
Coercion at Check-in At check-in, I explicitly rejected the additional charges, stating I had only two bags for two travelers. The XXXX agent refused to acknowledge the issue and told me I would be denied boarding if I did not pay immediately. I was forced to make the payment under extreme pressure and threat of missing my international flight. This was not a voluntary purchase, but a coerced and unauthorized transaction. Just because I paid these charges does not mean I actually checked in those disputed bags or that the charges were legitimate. The charges were imposed through duress, making them invalid, unlawful, and unenforceable under the Fair Credit Billing Act.

Barclays Obligations Under FCBA Barclays is obligated to conduct a reasonable and good-faith investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). In this case, the only documentation XXXX provided was a receipt showing that I paid, which I have never disputed. However, the receipt is not proof that the services were actually provided. It simply confirms that I was charged under duress for baggage I never checked in. 
Barclays failure to require XXXX to produce actual proof of service such as baggage tag numbers, weight records, or check-in logs amounts to a negligent and incomplete investigation and violates the intent of the FCBA. A merchant 's invoice or receipt alone does not meet the threshold of evidence when the dispute concerns whether the services were ever delivered.
